medical_history.history_of_present_illness: A 36-year-old man suffering from cystic fibrosis was admitted to our hospital in June 2009 for a heart and bilateral lung transplantation. He had a first lung transplantation in 1997 but had chronic rejection since 2001 in spite of several immunosuppressive regimens. His medical history also included renal failure (hemodialysis since 2007), diabetes mellitus, and airway colonization with a multiresistant Pseudomonas aeruginosa. Short antifungal therapy courses had been prescribed over the past years for various yeasts (Candida spp.) and molds (Aspergillus, Paecilomyces, and Fusarium species) colonizing his respiratory tract, but there was no evidence of previous isolation of a Scopulariopsis species.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Cystic fibrosis, first lung transplantation in 1997, chronic rejection since 2001, renal failure (hemodialysis since 2007), diabetes mellitus, airway colonization with a multiresistant Pseudomonas aeruginosa, colonization with various yeasts (Candida spp.) and molds (Aspergillus, Paecilomyces, and Fusarium species).

laboratory_examinations.findings: Increased slowly, reaching 20 x 10^9 cells/liter by day 8 postsurgery

laboratory_examinations.procedure_name: Fungal cultures of bilateral pleural specimens
laboratory_examinations.findings: Revealed restricted, white and velvety colonies after 6 days of incubation. Microscopic examination revealed branched, hyaline, regular, and septate but sterile hyphae
Positive. Direct smear examination revealed several filamentous and septate hyphae with intercalary chlamydospore-like cells
High MICs for all antifungal agents tested (8 μg/ml for amphotericin B, 4 μg/ml for caspofungin and terbinafine, and more than 8 μg/ml for itraconazole, voriconazole, and posaconazole)

radiographic_examinations.changes_over_time: Persistence of bilateral pleural effusions associated with pneumothorax and a slight pneumomediastinum on day 23

other_tests.procedure_name: Direct microscopic examination of pleural samples
other_tests.findings: Several regular, hyaline, and branched septate hyphae on direct examination
Highlighted pseudomembranes on the surgery scar

other_tests.procedure_name: Sequencing the D1/D2 region of the 28S rDNA
other_tests.findings: 99.6% similarity with Microascus cirrosus reference strain CBS 217.31 and 96% with Scopulariopsis brevicaulis strain IFM 54315
